threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check train times on your phone instead of asking the barista, and your eye twitches when someone says 'night tube.'
Saw a bloke on the 196 last night arguing with a lamp post. Kept insisting it knew where to find the best jollof rice. Absolute scenes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple having a picnic at the U-Bahn station while waiting for a bus that never came. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you stumble upon a techno party in a playground at 2 AM, complete with makeshift bars and glow sticks. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're from here when they casually mention a BART delay like it's the weather, but a 'transit breakdown' just makes them roll their eyes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing violin on the 6 bus, then a lady started singing along loudly. Muni turned into a mini concert, only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's just for partying; they miss the quiet mornings at local bakeries, where the real community brews over coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily at the grocery store, still amazed by the number of varieties of olives and asking what to do with them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Underground.' Everyone here knows it's just the Overground after a while.
Caught a glimpse of two mates sharing a burrito and laughing so hard on the 133, nearly missed their stop at Coldharbour Lane. Classic Brixton night out.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last night, I heard a group of kids turn a bus stop into a stage, dancing to their own beats while waiting for the delayed 104. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a Kotti kebab stand where live music blares and locals debate the best falafel toppings at 3 AM. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they know to stock up on snacks before a BART closure; it’s always the same story on Fridays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a guy with a pet parrot in a basket boarded the 38. The parrot squawked louder than the engine. Only in the Tenderloin, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists don’t realize the locals actually avoid Park Güell. Too many people, overpriced tapas, and it’s not even the best view of the city.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always hold their map upside down, looking bewildered at the closest metro entrance, while locals breeze by like they’re in a race.
